description: A host auto-granting an MCP tool broad filesystem, network, or shell access beyond what its declared function needs.
body: Failure mode: a tool that only needs to read one directory is granted full filesystem or arbitrary shell access because the host approved its request without scoping, so a buggy or malicious tool can reach far beyond its purpose. Detection signals: granted scope far exceeds the tool's declared inputs/effects; consent log shows blanket approval; an out-of-scope operation succeeds in testing. Do not auto-approve broad scopes for convenience. Safer fallback: map the tool's declared schema to the minimum rights, require explicit host consent for the narrow scope, and deny operations outside it. Risk boundary: escalate any tool requesting unrestricted shell or filesystem access to a human rather than granting it.
